rationale: I chose 16 practical, practitioner-facing subspecialties to cover the clinical spectrum clinicians and trialists use to describe themselves, balancing breadth (medical, surgical, diagnostic, perioperative) with depth (organ systems and cross-cutting fields like oncology and infectious disease). The list errs toward clinical identities (Cardiology, Oncology, Surgery) rather than research-method labels, and keeps adjacent public-health, basic-biology, and chemistry topics out of scope. I deliberately kept Radiology and Pathology separate (they are distinct clinical-professional communities) and kept Psychiatry as its own clinical specialty rather than splitting behavioral neuroscience — a controversial split I avoided to keep cards recognizable for clinicians and researchers alike.

subspecialties.name: Oncology
subspecialties.tagline: Cancer diagnosis, medical oncology, radiation & trials
covers: solid tumor and hematologic cancer studies, systemic therapy trials, radiation oncology, survivorship
examples: phase II/III chemotherapy trials, targeted therapy biomarker studies, tumor board case series
explicitly_excludes: Hematology — blood disorders without malignant focus
Biology — preclinical tumor biology belongs to Biology

subspecialties.name: Cardiology
subspecialties.tagline: Heart disease, interventional cardiology, heart failure research
covers: ischemic heart disease, arrhythmia, heart failure, interventional/stents, device trials
examples: randomized PCI trials, heart-failure drug trials, electrophysiology cohort studies
explicitly_excludes: Cardiac surgery — falls under Surgery
Vascular medicine — included here for clinical cardiology but large vascular-only trials may map to Surgery

subspecialties.name: Neurology
subspecialties.tagline: Stroke, neurodegeneration, epilepsy, clinical neuro studies
covers: acute stroke care, MS, Parkinson’s, dementia, epilepsy, neuroimmunology clinical work
examples: stroke thrombolysis RCTs, MS therapy trials, dementia progression cohorts
explicitly_excludes: Neurosurgery — operative interventions are under Surgery
Psychiatry — mood and psychotic disorders belong to Psychiatry

subspecialties.name: Surgery
subspecialties.tagline: General and specialty operative care, outcomes, perioperative medicine
covers: operative techniques, surgical oncology, vascular/orthopedic/thoracic procedures, perioperative outcomes
examples: randomized trials of surgical approaches, device implantation studies, surgical cohort outcomes
explicitly_excludes: Anesthesiology — perioperative anesthesia and analgesia studies map to Anesthesiology
Oncology — non-operative cancer medical/radiation studies remain in Oncology

subspecialties.name: Pediatrics
subspecialties.tagline: Child health, neonatal, pediatric subspecialty clinical studies
covers: neonatology, pediatric infectious disease, congenital disorders, vaccine trials in children
examples: pediatric vaccine RCTs, neonatal intensive care cohort studies, congenital cardiology follow-ups
explicitly_excludes: Obstetrics — maternal/fetal medicine goes to other clinical categories when applicable
Adult-focused subspecialties like Cardiology or Oncology when the population is adult

subspecialties.name: Radiology
subspecialties.tagline: Diagnostic imaging, interventional radiology, imaging biomarkers
covers: CT/MRI/ultrasound imaging studies, imaging-guided interventions, diagnostic accuracy and AI-imaging tools
examples: diagnostic performance papers, image-guided ablation trials, radiomics studies
explicitly_excludes: Pathology — tissue-based diagnostics remain in Pathology
Machine-learning methods with no clinical imaging application — may map to Computing or Biology

subspecialties.name: Infectious Disease
subspecialties.tagline: Clinical infectious diseases, antimicrobial trials, outbreak response
covers: antimicrobial therapy trials, vaccine effectiveness, hospital epidemiology, emerging pathogens
examples: COVID therapeutic RCTs, HIV treatment guidelines, antimicrobial stewardship studies
explicitly_excludes: Public Health — population-level epidemiology and surveillance maps to Public Health
Microbiology/Biology — lab-based pathogen discovery belongs to Biology

subspecialties.name: Pathology
covers: tissue diagnostics, immunohistochemistry, molecular pathology, diagnostic concordance studies
examples: biomarker validation on clinical specimens, autopsy series, diagnostic criteria papers
explicitly_excludes: Radiology — image-based diagnostics map to Radiology
Biology — basic molecular mechanism work is in Biology

subspecialties.name: Pulmonology
subspecialties.tagline: Lung disease, critical respiratory care, pulmonary trials
covers: COPD, asthma, interstitial lung disease, ARDS, ventilator management and respiratory critical care
examples: inhaled therapy RCTs, ARDS management trials, pulmonary function cohort studies
explicitly_excludes: Critical Care — if primarily ICU systems-level research, consider cross-mapping to Surgery or Hospital Medicine
Infectious Disease — infectious pneumonias remain Infectious Disease when primary focus

subspecialties.name: Gastroenterology
subspecialties.tagline: GI/liver disease, endoscopy, hepatology clinical studies
covers: IBD, liver disease, GI cancers, endoscopic techniques and outcomes
examples: endoscopic procedural trials, hepatitis antiviral clinical trials, IBD biologic studies
explicitly_excludes: Surgery — operative GI procedures belong to Surgery
Oncology — GI cancers focused on medical/radiation therapy remain Oncology

subspecialties.name: Hematology
subspecialties.tagline: Blood disorders, coagulopathy, transfusion and non-malignant hematology
covers: anemias, coagulopathies, transfusion medicine, benign hematologic disorders and some overlap with blood-cancer trials
examples: anticoagulation trials, transfusion thresholds studies, hemoglobinopathy cohorts
explicitly_excludes: Oncology — hematologic malignancies and leukemia/lymphoma trials map to Oncology
Pathology — lab hematopathology methods map to Pathology

subspecialties.name: Anesthesiology
subspecialties.tagline: Perioperative care, anesthesia, pain management, critical care anesthesia
covers: general and regional anesthesia, analgesia trials, perioperative outcomes and airway management
examples: analgesic regimen RCTs, airway device trials, perioperative morbidity studies
explicitly_excludes: Pain-focused outpatient neurology/psychiatry — chronic pain with behavioral/neuromodulation aspects may map to Neurology or Psychiatry
Surgery — operative technique trials remain Surgery

subspecialties.name: Endocrinology
subspecialties.tagline: Diabetes, metabolic disease, endocrine disorders clinical research
covers: diabetes management and devices, thyroid disease, metabolic bone disease, endocrine clinical trials
examples: glucose-lowering drug RCTs, thyroid disease cohort studies, endocrine surgical outcomes linked to Surgery
explicitly_excludes: Primary care/Preventive Medicine — lifestyle/population interventions map to Public Health
Biology — molecular endocrinology belongs to Biology

subspecialties.name: Nephrology
subspecialties.tagline: Kidney disease, dialysis, transplant nephrology clinical studies
covers: CKD progression, dialysis modalities, transplant outcomes, electrolyte disorders and AKI
examples: dialysis modality comparative trials, CKD drug trials, transplant immunosuppression cohorts
explicitly_excludes: Transplant surgery — operative transplant procedures map to Surgery
Immunology/basic transplant biology — maps to Biology when preclinical

subspecialties.name: Dermatology
subspecialties.tagline: Skin disease, dermatologic procedures, cutaneous oncology
covers: inflammatory skin disease, dermatologic surgery, topical/systemic therapies, skin cancer clinical work
examples: psoriasis biologic trials, melanoma clinical studies, dermatologic device trials
explicitly_excludes: Oncology — systemic cancer therapy trials for melanoma map to Oncology
Pathology — dermatopathology methods map to Pathology

subspecialties.name: Psychiatry
subspecialties.tagline: Mood, psychotic, and behavioral disorders; clinical psychopharmacology
covers: clinical trials of psychotropics, psychotherapy studies, neuropsychiatric disorder management
examples: antidepressant RCTs, CBT intervention trials, schizophrenia cohort and outcome studies
explicitly_excludes: Neurology — neurodegenerative and seizure disorders remain Neurology when primary focus
Public Health — population mental-health epidemiology maps to Public Health

uncovered_concerns: Critical Care/ICU: considered as a stand-alone subspecialty but split across Surgery, Anesthesiology, Pulmonology and Emergency domains in practice; kept distributed to match clinician identity.
Emergency Medicine: important clinically but small distinct research share and heavy overlap with Trauma/Surgery and Infectious Disease — deferred to cross-filter rather than a main tile.
Reproductive/Obstetrics & Gynecology: debated inclusion vs. keeping a separate women’s health tile; many papers are subsumed under relevant organ-system specialties or Public Health, so omitted here.
Primary Care / General Practice: large clinical audience but its papers often live in Public Health, implementation science, or specialty-specific trials; excluded to avoid overlap.
Digital health / Clinical AI: high-growth cross-cutting area but not a clinician self-title; these papers should be discoverable via method/topic tags rather than a specialty tile.
